Title: An oxygen-sensing mechanism for angiosperm adaptation to altitude

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FLUORESCENT IN BLUE LIGHT protein; negative regulator of tetrapyrrole/chlorophyll biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess free protochlorophyllide is phototoxic and can generate singlet oxygen/ROS upon illumination
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FLU represses protochlorophyllide accumulation; loss of FLU causes unrestrained high Pchlide under hypoxia and epistasis over prt6
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in etiolated seedlings; transcript and protein increase under hypoxia/low oxygen via ERFVIIs; lower in high-altitude accessions at 21% O2
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: flu mutant analysis; prt6 flu double mutant; qRT–PCR; western blot; ChIP of ERFVII binding to FLU locus; metabolite profiling of Pchlide
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GluTR; POR; CHL27; Pchlide-containing inactivation complex
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient oxygen concentration (5–27% O2); altitude (48 m vs 2,479 m a.s.l.); etiolated dark-grown seedlings
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLU is a central oxygen-sensing output that represses protochlorophyllide accumulation in etiolated seedlings. 2. ERFVIIs positively regulate FLU transcript/protein accumulation, and stabilized ERFVIIs act upstream of FLU. 3. Altitudinal clines in FLU expression correlate with oxygen sensitivity and protochlorophyllide homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune chlorophyll precursor homeostasis and altitude adaptation in crops by adjusting oxygen-sensing output
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced FLU can increase free protochlorophyllide and photo-oxidative ROS risk upon illumination
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FLU sequences were identical between Col-0 and Sha, indicating altitude adaptation is not due to coding variation in FLU but altered upstream oxygen sensing

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Committed entry step to tetrapyrrole synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces glutamyl-tRNA to glutamate-1-semialdehyde, leading to 5-aminolevulinic acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for protochlorophyllide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step; repressed through FLU-mediated inactivation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess is phototoxic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HEMA1 provides precursor flux for Pchlide synthesis, but its transcript was not regulated by ERFVIIs in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in etiolated seedlings; not detectably oxygen/ERFVII regulated here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT–PCR; pathway model; ChIP negative result for HRE2 at HEMA1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HEMA1 encodes the major GluTR catalysing the first committed tetrapyrrole step. 2. FLU inhibits GluTR activity to suppress protochlorophyllide accumulation. 3. Unlike several other tetrapyrrole genes, HEMA1 transcript was not regulated by ERFVIIs in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway component mechanistically tested in Results, despite absence of direct oxygen-responsive transcriptional regulation.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Mg-protoporphyrin IX monomethylester cyclase component CHL27
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tetrapyrrole synthesis enzyme; cyclase complex component
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in the cyclase step converting Mg-protoporphyrin monomethylester toward protochlorophyllide formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cyclized Mg-porphyrin intermediate en route to protochlorophyllide
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream intermediate and participates in FLU/POR/Pchlide inactivation complex affecting protochlorophyllide homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess is phototoxic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHL27 is negatively regulated by ERFVIIs and contributes to the inactivation complex controlling Pchlide synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in etiolated seedlings; transcript decreases under hypoxia as ERFVII activity increases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT–PCR; ChIP showing HRE2 association with CHL27 locus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHL27 is part of the tetrapyrrole synthesis machinery linked to FLU-mediated repression of GluTR. 2. ERFVIIs negatively regulate CHL27 expression. 3. HRE2 binds the CHL27 locus, supporting direct oxygen-sensing control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied mainly as part of the FLU-dependent inactivation complex logic rather than as the principal causal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal reduction step from protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in a light-dependent reaction using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Consumes target metabolite protochlorophyllide and helps bind/sequester free Pchlide in the inactivation complex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess is phototoxic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PORA expression is negatively regulated by ERFVIIs; stabilized ERFVIIs reduce PORA transcript and lower Pchlide accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in etiolated seedlings; higher in high-altitude accessions at 21% O2; lower under hypoxia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT–PCR; ChIP with HRE2 binding; transgenic ERFVII stabilization in Sha; western blot with anti-POR (combined POR signal)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient oxygen concentration; altitude; etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PORA is an ERFVII-repressed tetrapyrrole gene in etiolated seedlings. 2. Its expression is higher in high-altitude accessions under normoxia and decreases under low oxygen. 3. Altering ERFVII stability in Sha changes PORA expression together with protochlorophyllide levels.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: POR proteins also physically participate in the FLU–Pchlide–CHL27 inactivation complex.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal reduction step from protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in a light-dependent reaction using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Consumes target metabolite protochlorophyllide and contributes to free Pchlide buffering/inactivation complex formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess is phototoxic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PORB expression is negatively regulated by ERFVIIs; stabilized ERFVIIs reduce PORB transcript and lower Pchlide accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in etiolated seedlings; higher in high-altitude accessions at 21% O2; lower under hypoxia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT–PCR; ChIP with HRE2 binding; transgenic ERFVII stabilization in Sha; western blot with anti-POR (combined POR signal)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient oxygen concentration; altitude; etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PORB is an ERFVII-repressed tetrapyrrole gene in etiolated seedlings. 2. Its transcript abundance tracks altitude and oxygen conditions opposite to FLU. 3. Manipulation of ERFVII stability alters PORB expression and protochlorophyllide homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PORA and PORB were the two POR genes expressed in etiolated seedlings examined in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates Mg-protoporphyrin IX to Mg-protoporphyrin IX monomethyl ester
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream intermediate for protochlorophyllide synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess is phototoxic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHLM transcript is negatively regulated by ERFVIIs and is higher in high-altitude accessions under normoxia
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in etiolated seedlings; lower under hypoxia; higher in high-altitude accessions at 21% O2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT–PCR; ChIP showing HRE2 association with CHLM locus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient oxygen concentration; altitude; etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHLM is oxygen/ERFVII responsive in etiolated seedlings. 2. ERFVIIs negatively regulate CHLM expression. 3. CHLM expression shows altitude-associated variation consistent with altered oxygen-sensing sensitivity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously shown to be oxygen-sensing regulated and incorporated here as part of the tetrapyrrole output module.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group VII AP2/ERF transcription factors with N-terminal Met-Cys degron
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Act as substrates of the PCO branch of the PRT6 N-degron pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Tetrapyrrole/chlorophyll precursor homeostasis and hypoxia-responsive transcription
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FLU; CHL27; PORA; PORB; CHLM; ADH1; PDC1; PGB1; SUS4; PCO1; CRU1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of FLU and hypoxia genes; transcriptional repression of CHL27, PORA, PORB and CHLM; oxygen-dependent proteolysis via N-degron pathway controls regulator abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilized ERFVIIs lower protochlorophyllide accumulation; loss of all ERFVIIs largely abolishes oxygen-dependent suppression of Pchlide and reduces FLU expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protochlorophyllide is a chlorophyll precursor but free excess is phototoxic and causes ROS under light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pentuple erfVII mutant; individual ERFVII mutants; native promoter-driven WT/Cys2Ala stabilized transgenes; ChIP-qPCR; qRT–PCR; western blot of HRE2-HA; Pchlide profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters for RAP2.3 and HRE2 transgenes in Arabidopsis; 35S promoter used for HRE2-HA line in some occupancy/protein analyses
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient oxygen concentration (hypoxia to hyperoxia); altitude; proteasome inhibition by bortezomib
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Atmospheric oxygen concentration via PCO/PRT6 N-degron pathway; possibly nitric oxide as contextual pathway component
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in etiolated seedlings; occupancy and abundance higher in low-altitude Col-0 than high-altitude Sha at 21% O2; increased activity under 15% O2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERFVIIs are required for oxygen-dependent regulation of protochlorophyllide accumulation in etiolated Arabidopsis seedlings. 2. They directly bind and regulate FLU and several tetrapyrrole/hypoxia genes. 3. Altitude adaptation is associated with altered ERFVII accumulation/activity, with lower normoxic ERFVII activity in high-altitude accessions.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PCO1; PCO2; PRT6; ATE; RAP2.3; HRE2
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive stabilization enhances hypoxia gene expression and strongly represses Pchlide accumulation, potentially perturbing normal developmental oxygen signaling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key leverage point for engineering altitude adaptation and oxygen-sensing sensitivity in crops
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master oxygen-sensing regulators linking ambient O2 to Pchlide homeostasis and hypoxia outputs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FLU, ADH1, PDC1, PGB1, SUS4; represses CHL27, PORA, PORB, CHLM; no clear regulation of HEMA1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RAP2.3 had the largest individual effect on Pchlide among ERFVIIs

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RAP2.3 has a predominant role among ERFVIIs; stabilized RAP2.3(Cys2Ala) lowers Pchlide and increases FLU while reducing PORA/PORB in Sha
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll precursor; excess causes photo-oxidative damage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single mutant effect; native promoter transgene in WT and Cys2Ala form; ChIP-qPCR at FLU locus; qRT–PCR; Pchlide assay
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Oxygen sensing via PCO/PRT6 N-degron pathway
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAP2.3 directly binds the FLU locus. 2. RAP2.3 stabilization strongly suppresses protochlorophyllide accumulation. 3. RAP2.3 appears to make the largest individual ERFVII contribution to this trait.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FLU; indirectly/overall represses Pchlide accumulation

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: HRE2-HA abundance is higher in Col-0 than Sha at 21.2 kPa O2; occupancy increases under 15% O2 and after proteasome inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Oxygen-dependent proteolysis controls HRE2 abundance and chromatin occupancy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilized HRE2(Cys2Ala) reduces Pchlide and alters FLU/PORA/PORB expression; wild-type HRE2 occupancy at targets is lower in high-altitude Sha than Col-0 under normoxia
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll precursor; excess causes photooxidative ROS upon illumination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Native promoter WT and Cys2Ala transgenes; 35S:HRE2-3xHA introgression; ChIP-qPCR; western blot; qRT–PCR; Pchlide assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native HRE2 promoter; 35S promoter for HRE2-3xHA line
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient oxygen concentration; altitude; bortezomib treatment
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Oxygen via PCO/PRT6 N-degron pathway; proteasome activity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HRE2 directly occupies FLU, CHL27, CHLM, PORA and PORB loci and hypoxia-related genes. 2. HRE2 abundance and target occupancy are lower in high-altitude Sha than low-altitude Col-0 at normoxia. 3. Stabilizing HRE2 suppresses protochlorophyllide accumulation via altered FLU and POR expression.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FLU; CHL27; CHLM; PORA; PORB; ADH1 and other hypoxia genes; PCO1; CRU1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct oxygen-responsive regulator linking altitude-sensitive O2 sensing to Pchlide homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FLU and hypoxia genes; represses CHL27/CHLM/PORA/PORB overall

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: E3 ligase in oxygen-sensing N-degron pathway
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: PCO branch of the PRT6 N-degron proteolysis pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PRT6 stabilizes ERFVIIs, increases FLU, reduces Pchlide, and is epistatic upstream of FLU
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess is phototoxic
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: prt6 mutants; prt6 flu double mutant; prt6Sha introgression; Pchlide assay; qRT–PCR; western blot
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Acts downstream of PCO-catalysed cysteine oxidation and arginylation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRT6 negatively controls ERFVII stability in the oxygen-sensing pathway. 2. prt6 mutants exhibit low protochlorophyllide because ERFVIIs are stabilized. 3. Introgression of prt6 into Sha shows the high-altitude phenotype remains responsive to upstream oxygen-sensing manipulation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive ERFVII stabilization may alter broader hypoxia/stress transcriptional programs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Proteolytic control of oxygen-responsive transcription impacting tetrapyrrole flux
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream switch determining ERFVII abundance and thus FLU/POR expression and Pchlide levels

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Oxygen sensor enzyme controlling regulator stability
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: PCO branch of the PRT6 N-degron pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Oxidizes N-terminal cysteine of ERFVIIs using molecular oxygen, promoting their degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increasing PCO2 activity elevates Pchlide by destabilizing active ERFVIIs; effect requires PRT6
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of chlorophyll; excess is phototoxic
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S:PCO2 overexpression in Col-0 and prt6 background; Pchlide assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PCO2 is an oxygen-dependent upstream sensor in the ERFVII pathway. 2. Overexpression of PCO2 increases protochlorophyllide by promoting ERFVII turnover. 3. This effect is blocked in prt6, confirming pathway order.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced ERFVII turnover may dampen hypoxia responses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream O2-sensing enzyme that sets ERFVII stability and thereby Pchlide output
